FOREWORD
This pamphlet, which is comprised of ex-
cerpts taken from the Insurance Code as en-
acted by the 1950 Legislature and effective
September 1, 1950, is furnished you as a service
facility by your Insurance Department .
Many changes are incorporated in this Code
and it is our intention that by setting forth
herein specific Articles , Sections and Sub-
sections that are applicable either primarily or
solely to Agents , your becoming familiar with
such changes may be facilitated to a great
degree . We sincerely hope that all who study
this pamphlet will be benefited thereby and
that the resulting improvement in the quality
of performance of Agents will have been ele-
vated to a level never before surpassed within
Chis Commonwealth .
Sincerely ,
SPALDING SOUTHALL

classification.title: Insurance Law Related To Agents 1950
classification.summary: This statute defines the requirements for an insurable interest in life insurance contracts. It states that a person can procure insurance on their own life for any beneficiary, but for insurance on another individual's life, the benefits must be payable to the insured or someone with an insurable interest at the time the contract was made. It further defines insurable interest as a substantial interest based on love and affection for close blood or legal relations, or a lawful and substantial economic interest for other persons, distinguishing it from an interest that would arise or be enhanced by the insured's death or injury. It also specifies insurable interest for business partnership or close corporation share contracts.

However, it allows for consideration of risk nature, plan of insurance, and expenses in determining classes.
